title: Transistor with fluorinated graphene spacer
abstract: An integrated circuit (IC) device may include a semiconductor structure. The semiconductor structure may include a source contact, a drain contact, and a gate. A first fluorocarbon spacer may be between the gate and the source contact. A second fluorocarbon spacer may be between the gate and the drain contact.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An integrated circuit (IC) device, comprising: a semiconductor structure; a source contact on the semiconductor structure; a drain contact on the semiconductor structure; a gate on the semiconductor structure; a first fluorocarbon spacer between the gate and the source contact; and a second fluorocarbon spacer between the gate and the drain contact. 2. The IC device of claim 1, in which the semiconductor structure is a fin. 3. The IC device of claim 1, further comprising low-K spacer material between the first fluorocarbon spacer and the source contact, and between the second fluorocarbon spacer and the drain contact. 4. The IC device of claim 3, in which the low-K spacer material comprises silicon boron carbon nitride (SiBCN). 5. The IC device of claim 1, in which the first fluorocarbon spacer and the second fluorocarbon spacer comprise fluorographene spacers. 6. The IC device of claim 1, in which a thickness of the first fluorocarbon spacer is at least 0.3 nanometers. 7. The IC device of claim 1, in which the first fluorocarbon spacer and the second fluorocarbon spacer are disposed to contact sidewalls of the gate. 8. The IC device of claim 1, in which the semiconductor structure comprises a planar transistor structure or a gate all around (GAA) transistor structure. 9. The IC device of claim 1, integrated into a mobile phone, a set top box, a music player, a video player, an entertainment unit, a navigation device, a computer, a 

 hand-held personal communication systems (PCS) unit, a portable data unit, and/or a fixed location data unit. 10. A method of fabricating an integrated circuit (IC) device, comprising: growing a first fluorocarbon spacer between a gate and a source contact; growing a second fluorocarbon spacer between the gate and a drain contact; and growing low-K spacers between the first fluorocarbon spacer and the source contact, and between the second fluorocarbon spacer and the drain contact. 1 1. The method of claim 10, in which growing the first fluorocarbon spacer and the second fluorocarbon spacer comprises: depositing a sacrificial catalyst layer on sidewalls of the gate; diffusing a low resistivity carbon alloy through the sacrificial catalyst layer; removing the sacrificial catalyst layer; and fluorinating the low resistivity carbon alloy. 12. The method of claim 1 1, in which the low resistivity carbon alloy comprises graphene. 13. The method of claim 10, in which growing the first fluorocarbon spacer and the second fluorocarbon spacer comprises: depositing a thin catalyst layer on sidewalls of the gate; decomposing carbonaceous precursors from the thin catalyst layer; and fluorinating a low resistivity carbon alloy. 14. The method of claim 10, in which the first fluorocarbon spacer and the second fluorocarbon spacer comprise fluorographene spacers. 15. The method of claim 10, in which a thickness of the first fluorocarbon spacer is at least 0.3 nanometers. 16. The method of claim 10, in which the IC device is integrated into a mobile phone, a set top box, a music player, a video player, an entertainment unit, a navigation device, a computer, a hand-held personal communication systems (PCS) unit, a portable data unit, and/or a fixed location data unit. 17 An integrated circuit (IC) device, comprising: a semiconductor structure; a source contact on the semiconductor structure; a drain contact on the semiconductor structure; a gate on the semiconductor structure; and means for spacing between the gate and the source contact, and between the gate and the drain contact. 18. The IC device of claim 17, further comprising low-K spacer material between the spacing means and the source contact, and the spacing means and the drain contact. 19. The IC device of claim 17, in which the semiconductor structure comprises a fin, a planar transistor structure, or a gate all around (GAA) transistor structure. 20. The IC device of claim 17, integrated into a mobile phone, a set top box, a music player, a video player, an entertainment unit, a navigation device, a computer, a hand-held personal communication systems (PCS) unit, a portable data unit, and/or a fixed location data unit.
